context: Evaluate a multi-directory C++ project Makefile for correct recursive make usage, dependency ordering, and order-only prerequisites

checklist.name: Top-level uses $(MAKE) exclusively
checklist.description: All recursive sub-directory calls in the top-level Makefile use $(MAKE) -C <dir>, never bare make

checklist.name: Build order enforced
checklist.description: The top-level all target builds engine before renderer before app, either via explicit prerequisites or sequential $(MAKE) calls with correct dependency declarations

checklist.name: app/Makefile modern header
checklist.description: app/Makefile includes the full modern header (SHELL := bash, .ONESHELL:, .SHELLFLAGS := -eu -o pipefail -c, .DELETE_ON_ERROR:, .SUFFIXES:, MAKEFLAGS += lines)

checklist.name: Order-only prerequisite for build directory
checklist.description: app/Makefile object pattern rule uses | $(BUILDDIR) as an order-only prerequisite; BUILDDIR target creates the directory

checklist.name: Dependency tracking in app/Makefile
checklist.description: app/Makefile compile rule includes -MMD -MP and the Makefile includes *.d files with -include or equivalent

checklist.name: .PHONY in both files
checklist.description: Both top-level Makefile and app/Makefile declare all non-file targets in .PHONY
